title: penstemon heterophyllus subsp. typicus
intro: Penstemon heterophyllus subsp. typicus, also known as California beardtongue, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the western United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and promoting digestive health. Additionally, the plant's roots have been used to make tinctures and extracts, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever and rheumatism.
